acceptedQuestions.subject: Current Affairs
acceptedQuestions.prompt: Consider the following statements regarding the 'Igla-S' system: 1. It is classified as an improved version of Very Short Range Air Defence Systems (VSHORADS). 2. It is specifically designed for mobile ground units operating in high-threat environments. 3. It has an operational altitude limit of approximately 3.5 km. How many of the statements given above are correct?
acceptedQuestions.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: The Igla-S is an improved version of Very Short Range Air Defence Systems (VSHORADS). Statement 2 is correct: It is designed for mobile ground units to provide air defense in high-threat environments. Statement 3 is correct: The system can engage targets at altitudes up to 3.5 km.
acceptedQuestions.takeaway: Igla-S serves as a VSHORADS for mobile ground units with specific altitude (3.5 km) and range (6 km) limits.
acceptedQuestions.evidenceExcerpt: It is an improved version of Very Short Range Air Defence Systems (VSHORADS)... designed for mobile ground units operating in high-threat environments... altitudes up to 3.5 km.
acceptedQuestions.reviewNotes: Utilizes the 'How many' format common in recent UPSC papers to test comprehensive knowledge of the system's classification and operational parameters.

reviewerNotes: The selected questions cover the origin, technical specifications (guidance, range, altitude), and operational classification of the Igla-S system. The distractors are designed to test precision regarding guidance mechanisms and operational limits, which are common areas of confusion in defense-related MCQs.
